;<span id="Minimum_information_principle">minimum information principle</span>
:principle of effective learning which says that simple [[Glossary:Element|elements]] formulated for [[Glossary:Active_recall|active recall]] bring much better learning results than complex [[Glossary:Element|elements]] even though one complex element may be equivalent to a large number of simpler [[Glossary:Element|elements]].
:Read more:
:*[https://super-memory.com/articles/20rules.htm 20 rules of formulating knowledge]
:*[[Incremental_learning#Minimum_information_principle|Incremental learning and the minimum information principle]]
